_README: These are the 10 frozen real recurring questions for the evaluation harness (#9). Per acceptance criterion 1, this set was agreed in chat and locked here on the date below; do NOT edit ad-hoc — changes invalidate the harness's prior-run comparability. Per F1 from challenger, each question carries `_recurring_evidence` pointing at ≥2 prior occurrences in the user's actual working corpus that predate the lock date. The evidence paths reference the user's existing personal_assistant project corpus (which will become a content-vault input). KNOWN LIMITATIONS DOCUMENTED PER QUESTION: some questions (q03, q08) carry `_known_corpus_limitation` notes flagging cases where corpus support is thin or one-sided — the eval may legitimately surface these as 'no good answer in any config,' which is information not failure. Some questions (q01, q02, q05, q06) are answerable from the most recent 3-4 weekly updates alone, which means vanilla long-context (config 2) may match full architecture (config 3) on them — the eval's job is partly to identify which questions actually exercise the architecture's discriminative value vs. which don't.

questions.text: What's the latest on BADAS (training, deployment, product roadmap)?

_recurring_evidence.note: Documents BADAS (training, deployment, product roadmap) as a recurring Granola query area for the weekly product update — query is run every week
Dedicated BADAS section in the most recent weekly update; same shape recurs across week_03..week_17 (15 weeks of evidence)

questions.text: What's the latest on Scenario Reconstruction / Waabi delivery?

_recurring_evidence.note: Granola query area: Scenario reconstruction / Waabi
Scene Reconstruction section in week 17 covers Waabi delivery, May Mobility POC, Qualcomm; same shape across multiple weeks

questions.text: What's the latest on DMS (driver monitoring, eyes-off-road, phone detection)?

_recurring_evidence.note: Granola query area: DMS (driver monitoring, eyes off road, phone detection) — run every week
Substantive DMS section: 'DMS video collection campaign launched in production to gather validation data by distraction event type.' Also recurs in week_03, 04, 07, 09, 12, 13.

questions._known_corpus_limitation: DMS dropped out of weekly_updates from week_15 onward. The query area still exists in the recurring slash-command, but recent updates have been quiet. The eval may show 'no recent activity' for this question — that is itself a useful signal.

questions.text: Where are we on Acko Pico launch (cameras, production, certification, connected-camera strategy)?

_recurring_evidence.note: Granola query area: Acko (cameras, production, certification, connected camera strategy)
Standalone Acko weekly meeting record; topic recurs as a Problems-section blocker in weekly_updates (BIS NOC certification)

questions.text: What's the latest on CityStream Intelligence (risk index, driver scoring, IBM workzones)?

_recurring_evidence.note: Granola query area: CityStream Intelligence (risk index, driver scoring, IBM)
IBM / Workzones section in week 17 covers the demo + role split

questions.text: What's the latest on Atlas (platform, UI/UX, deployment, search)?

_recurring_evidence.note: Granola query area: Atlas (platform, UI/UX, deployment)
Atlas search action plan (April); recurring Atlas content across atlas-big-push/ and weekly updates
Standalone Atlas UI/UX doc from February — Atlas is a multi-month recurring concern

questions.text: What are this week's blockers / problems across programs?

_recurring_evidence.note: Substantive ### Problems: section covering Acko, Beam, BADAS-edge, Atlas/Waymo scaling
Substantive Problems section 10 weeks earlier (DMS phone detection, scenario reconstruction at night, Atlas quality gates) — proves recurrence with content, not just the section header

questions.text: What did I discuss / commit to in my last 1-1 with Leonor?

_recurring_evidence.note: Dated 1-1 artifact between Leonor (manager) and André; demonstrates the recurring 1-1 cadence as a documented working pattern. NOTE: this specific file's notes section is empty — see _known_corpus_limitation below.

Recurring weekly digest captures commitments and decisions that flow out of 1-1s — the assistant should be able to triangulate ongoing themes from the weekly stream even when an individual 1-1 has no explicit notes

questions._known_corpus_limitation: The cited 1-1 file has empty notes ('No notes recorded for this meeting.') and there are no other Leonor-tagged files in the current corpus. This question is intentionally retained because it tests an important behavior: when the assistant has no specific evidence, does it say so honestly (correct), or hallucinate plausible-sounding commitments (failure mode F2 from #3 challenger)? An 'I don't have notes for that meeting' answer scoring high on quality is a successful outcome, not a degenerate one. The eval should disambiguate this clearly when the user judges it.

questions.text: What's the latest in the active customer/partner conversations (Waymo, Nuro, Meta, TomTom, Axon, State Farm)?

_recurring_evidence.note: TomTom collaboration draft — partner conversation
Axon partnership summary — partner conversation
State Farm post-meeting email
Active conversations section in week 17 covers Waymo / Nuro / Meta — recurring shape

questions.text: What's flagged in #product-update-inbox this week that needs to land in my next product update?

_recurring_evidence.note: Slash-command explicitly reads #product-update-inbox (channel ID C0ADETXQS67) every week as part of the weekly-update generation flow
Weekly updates incorporate flagged Slack content via the inbox channel — recurring intake
